what_it_does: Stylesheet that defines the visual layout, color, typography, hover/focus states, and animations for the Counterpart welcome/landing screen and related studio chrome, including the Vi presence orb, prompt bar, project cards, phase/journey indicators, and next-step callout.
observations: Warm gold/bronze and parchment color palette implemented with layered gradients and a subtle dot-grid overlay.
Full-viewport fixed welcome container with z-index layering and scrollable inner content.
Animated Vi orb made of concentric rotating rings, a glowing core, and an 'Enter Studio' hover/focus caption.
Prompt bar with rounded pill shape, mic/send buttons, placeholder styling, and suggestion chips.
Featured project card uses a full-cover image with a dark gradient overlay, phase dots, and a Vi whisper callout.
Smaller project cards use thumbnail images, compact text blocks, and a dashed 'new project' variant.
Phase strip and journey bar components show progress with gold fills, connector lines, and pulsing active states.
Next-step callout at the bottom of workspaces repeats the gold orb motif and guidance pattern.
BEM-style class naming with component prefixes: welcome__, journey__, phase-strip, next-step.
CSS custom properties are used heavily, often with fallback values, but many variables are not defined in this file.
standouts: The Vi presence orb is treated as an interactive entry point with animated rings and a hover caption 'Enter Studio'.
The featured project card combines imagery, gradient overlay, phase dots, and a Vi whisper into one dense hero component.
The same file also contains the in-studio journey bar and next-step callout, extending the welcome visual language into the workspace.
conventions: BEM-like block-element naming with double underscores and modifiers.
CSS custom properties with fallback values for theming.
Focus-visible states paired with hover states for keyboard accessibility.
Decorative overlays generated with ::before and ::after pseudo-elements.
Consistent use of transitions, transforms, and box-shadow for interactive feedback.
Grid and flexbox layouts for component structure.
concerns: The file's scope exceeds its stated welcome-screen purpose by including journey and next-step components.
Many CSS variables (e.g., --ms-hairline, --r-pill, --accent-gold) are referenced but not defined locally, making the file hard to evaluate in isolation.
z-index values are sparse and arbitrary (e.g., z-index: 2 on .welcome), which may cause stacking issues elsewhere.
No media queries or responsive adjustments are present for smaller viewports.
Multiple infinite animations on the orb rings and active dots could affect performance on low-end devices.
The fixed inset welcome container with overflow-y: auto relies on inner padding that may clip content on very short viewports.
notes: This is the warm, gold-themed CSS for the Counterpart welcome landing and its related journey/next-step chrome, organized in BEM blocks with gradients, animations, and interactive hover states.
questions: Where are the referenced CSS custom properties (e.g., --ms-hairline, --r-pill, --accent-gold) defined, and what are their exact values?
Is the journey component intended for the welcome screen, or is it reused from another component's stylesheet?
What markup structure is expected for the Vi whisper and journey phase items?
Are project card images always present, and is there a fallback when they fail to load?
Is there a dark-mode or high-contrast variant of these styles?
What is the intended responsive behavior on narrow viewports?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is 'Welcome screen styles / Vi-led front-of-app,' which matches most of the content, but the file also contains in-studio journey and next-step components, so its actual scope is broader than the welcome screen alone.
apparent_themes: Warm, tactile parchment-and-gold creative workspace aesthetic
Progressive orientation through journey and phase indicators
intention_signals: It appears designed to create an inviting, assistant-fronted entry point that transitions smoothly into a project workspace, using a consistent visual language of a golden orb, progress indicators, and gentle guidance callouts to orient the user.
novelty: The Vi orb as a literal navigational presence with concentric animated rings and a hover 'Enter Studio' caption; the blending of landing-page hero styling with in-studio journey chrome within a single stylesheet.
contribution: A complete visual vocabulary for the welcome experience and related studio guidance surfaces, including the orb, prompt bar, project cards, phase/journey indicators, and next-step callout, along with their color, spacing, motion, and interaction patterns.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet for the Counterpart welcome screen and related studio chrome, implementing a warm gold-and-parchment BEM component system with gradients, animations, hover/focus states, and progress indicators.
intention_for_embedding: To render a Vi-led landing page and consistent in-studio guidance surfaces that feel like a creative assistant's workspace.
